macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: IMF Concludes 2026 Article IV Consultation with US, Praises 2% GDP Growth in 2025 Despite Shutdown
items.summary: The IMF highlighted strong US economic performance in 2025 with 2% GDP growth driven by productivity, despite policy shifts and a Q4 government shutdown. Projections show modest acceleration to 2.4% in 2026, with concerns over tariffs, debt at 123.9% of GDP, and Middle East war risks.

items.title: US Trade Deficit Widens to $57.3 Billion in February 2026, Up 4.9% from January
items.summary: The US goods and services trade deficit rose to $57.3 billion in February from $54.7 billion in January, with exports at $314.8 billion (up 4.2%) and imports at $372.1 billion (up 4.3%). Year-to-date, the deficit narrowed 54.8% from 2025 levels.

items.title: US Government Shutdown Ends with Deal Through January, Federal Workers Get Back Pay
items.summary: Parties reached a deal to reopen the government through January, providing back pay to federal workers and reversing layoffs. Democrats secure a Senate vote on Obamacare subsidies, while Trump pushes legal fight on spending authority.

items.title: Federal Reserve Cuts Rates by 0.25%, Ends Balance Sheet Reduction Amid Shutdown Data Limits
items.summary: The Fed lowered rates by 0.25% and ceased balance sheet reduction despite limited data from the government shutdown. Markets anticipate another cut in December, with focus on Powell's hints amid mixed inflation signals from recent CPI.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Tops $112,000 Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin has surged above $112,000 as traders increase risk bets ahead of major Federal Reserve events. The leading cryptocurrency continues to show resilience despite broader market fluctuations.

items.title: Solana Posts Blowout Week with 16% Gain, Outperforming Major Altcoins
items.summary: Solana has delivered exceptional weekly performance with a 16% increase, significantly outpacing Bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies as traders ramp up risk appetite.

items.title: XRP and Solana Emerge as Top Winners in Recent Rally
items.summary: XRP and Solana have become the biggest gainers in the cryptocurrency market, with both assets posting substantial gains as traders shift capital to altcoins.

items.title: Hyperliquid's Perpetuals Market Share Reaches Nearly 6%
items.summary: The decentralized perpetuals platform Hyperliquid has increased its market share to approximately 6% in March 2026, up significantly from 3.5% a year earlier, capturing volume from centralized exchanges.

items.title: Circle Launches Wrapped Bitcoin cirBTC Backed 1:1 by BTC
items.summary: Stablecoin issuer Circle has announced the upcoming launch of cirBTC, a wrapped Bitcoin token fully backed 1:1 by BTC and verifiable on-chain, designed to integrate with DeFi ecosystems.

items.title: Circle Faces Scrutiny Over $230M Stolen USDC Flow Through Bridge
items.summary: Blockchain investigator ZachXBT alleges that Circle remained inactive while a hacker funneled over $230 million in stolen USDC through its cross-chain bridge infrastructure.

items.title: SpaceX Files Confidential IPO, Eyes $75B Raise
items.summary: SpaceX has confidentially filed for an IPO seeking $75 billion in funding, with preparations underway for a potential June 2026 listing to support Starship and Starlink expansion.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Ubisoft Sued by French Consumer Group Over The Crew Shutdown
items.summary: UFC-Que Choisir is suing Ubisoft in court after the publisher shut down servers for The Crew, rendering purchased copies unplayable without warning. This lawsuit advances efforts to prevent publishers from destroying access to bought games.

items.title: Bungie Announces Major Marathon Updates Including Balance Changes and Nerfs
items.summary: Bungie revealed significant reworks for Marathon, such as knife damage changes, Bubble Shield nerfs, new Sentinel Shell, Season 1 updates, and controller optimizations amid reports of low player count.

items.title: Xbox Games Showcase to Feature Gears of War: E-Day Direct
items.summary: Microsoft's upcoming Xbox Games Showcase will include a Direct for Gears of War: E-Day, with discussions on a stacked 2026 lineup including Call of Duty, Forza Horizon 6, and potential delays for Fable.

items.title: Crimson Desert Receives Full Review After 140+ Hours
items.summary: A detailed review praises Crimson Desert's combat, progression, and content despite a slow start and mixed first impressions, rating various aspects after extensive play including main story and side activities.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Agrees to $14.2B Buyback of Apollo's Fab 34 Stake in Ireland
items.summary: Intel has agreed to repurchase Apollo's 49% stake in the Fab 34 joint venture in Ireland for $14.2 billion, with plans to issue $6.5 billion in new debt to fund the acquisition. The announcement drove Intel stock up 10%.

items.title: Baidu Robotaxi System Failure Traps Passengers in Wuhan
items.summary: A suspected system failure froze Baidu's robotaxis across Wuhan, trapping passengers and reportedly causing traffic disruptions and crashes.

items.title: Raspberry Pi Raises Prices Following Memory Cost Increases
items.summary: Raspberry Pi announced price increases ranging from $11.25 to $150, citing higher memory costs following December and February hikes. The company also unveiled a new 3GB Raspberry Pi 4 model priced at $83.75.

items.title: Super Micro Co-founder Pleads Not Guilty to Server Smuggling Charges
items.summary: Super Micro co-founder Yih-Shyan Liaw pleaded not guilty to US federal charges of helping smuggle billions of dollars' worth of Nvidia-powered servers to China.

items.title: Microsoft Releases Emergency Patch for Windows 11 Update Issues
items.summary: Microsoft released another emergency patch to address ongoing Windows 11 update problems, following previous acknowledgment of issues with the operating system.

items.title: NASA Launches Artemis II with Astronauts on First Crewed Lunar Mission in 50 Years
items.summary: NASA successfully launched four astronauts on Artemis II, marking the first crewed mission to fly around the moon since the Apollo program ended 50 years ago.
